examples.title: Evaluating Absolute Values
examples.content:
Evaluate: $|7|$, $|-7|$, $|0|$, $|-2.5|$.

- $|7| = 7$ (7 is already positive, distance from zero is 7)
- $|-7| = 7$ (7 is negative; take its opposite: $-(-7) = 7$)
- $|0| = 0$ (zero is zero distance from itself)
- $|-2.5| = 2.5$ (take opposite of negative: $-(-2.5) = 2.5$)

examples.title: Distance on a Number Line
examples.content:
Find the distance between -3 and 5 on a number line.

**Solution**: Distance $= |-3 - 5| = |-8| = 8$ units.

Alternatively: $|5 - (-3)| = |8| = 8$ units.

Both give the same answer because $|a - b| = |b - a|$.
